response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software craftsman with a long track record, but is a poor fit for this specific role. The position explicitly requires native-level Japanese proficiency, which the candidate does not have (languages listed are English, Romanian, and basic German). Beyond that hard blocker, the role centers on C for UNIX, embedded LAN-switch firmware, Linux kernel source modification, and networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3, switch chips, SDKs) — none of which appear in the candidate's recent work, which is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web/mobile development. There is some older C/C++ and embedded-adjacent experience, but it is not enough to offset the missing core requirements.
strengths: Deep, broad engineering experience including some C/C++ and embedded work (LabWindows/CVI, robotic arms)
Strong testing/engineering discipline (TDD, CI/CD, low-latency systems) and Linux familiarity
Fluent English for technical documentation and communication
concerns: No native-level Japanese — a stated hard requirement
No demonstrated networking-security or LAN-switch embedded firmware experience (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3)
No evidence of Linux kernel source-code modification; recent stack is web/JS, not C/UNIX
